prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Sweetie, I think the CPU is like the brain of the computer, dear. It's the part that makes everything work, like the heart of the machine. I'm not entirely sure how it does it all, but I know it's very important! It's like a tiny little boss that tells all the other parts what to do and when, and it makes sure everything runs smoothly. I remember when I was your age, we didn't even have these new-fangled computers, but I'm glad you're interested in learning about them!
